title: draba bruniifolia var. incana
intro: Draba bruniifolia var. incana, also known as mountain whitlowgrass, is a perennial plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that grows in alpine and subalpine regions. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Draba bruniifolia var. incana can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
